content:
# Disagreement Log -- Numbers 36 (NUM_036)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA

---

### 36:1 -- "heads of the fathers" vs. "heads of the ancestral households"; "drew near" vs. "came forward"; "son of Machir" vs. "the son of Machir"
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And the heads of the fathers of the clan of the sons of Gilead son of Machir, son of Manasseh, from the clans of the sons of Joseph, drew near and spoke before Moses and before the leaders, the heads of the fathers of the sons of Israel." |
| LOGOS | "And the heads of the fathers of the clan of the sons of Gilead, the son of Machir, the son of Manasseh, from the clans of the sons of Joseph, drew near and spoke before Moses and before the leaders, the heads of the fathers of the sons of Israel." |
| PNEUMA | "And the heads of the ancestral households of the clan of the sons of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh -- from the clans of the sons of Joseph -- came forward and spoke before Moses and before the leaders, the heads of the ancestral households of the sons of Israel." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "And the heads of the fathers of the clan of the sons of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh, from the clans of the sons of Joseph, drew near and spoke before Moses and before the leaders, the heads of the fathers of the sons of Israel." -- (1) "heads of the fathers" (EMET/LOGOS) is adopted over "heads of the ancestral households" (PNEUMA). The Hebrew ha'avot means "the fathers"; "ancestral households" is interpretive and adds a word not in the text. (2) "drew near" (EMET/LOGOS) for vayyiqr'vu is adopted over "came forward" (PNEUMA); qarav means "to draw near," and "drew near" is more literal. (3) LOGOS adds the article "the" before "son of Machir" and "son of Manasseh" (reading "the son of Machir, the son of Manasseh"); the Hebrew has ben without the article. EMET's rendering without "the" is adopted. (4) PNEUMA sets "from the clans of the sons of Joseph" off with em-dashes as a parenthetical; the Hebrew does not mark this as parenthetical, so the comma structure (EMET/LOGOS) is adopted.

---

### 36:2 -- "our brother" vs. "our kinsman"
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "...to give the inheritance of Zelophehad our brother to his daughters." |
| LOGOS | "...to give the inheritance of Zelophehad our brother to his daughters." |
| PNEUMA | "...to give the inheritance of Zelophehad our kinsman to his daughters." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "our brother" (EMET/LOGOS) is adopted. The Hebrew akhinu means literally "our brother." Zelophehad is not a literal brother but a clan relative; however, akh is used broadly in Hebrew for tribal kinsmen, and "brother" preserves the Hebrew term. PNEUMA's "kinsman" is interpretive, substituting the sense for the word.

---

### 36:3 -- "become [wives] to one of the sons" vs. "become wives to any of the sons" vs. "marry any of the sons"; "shall be taken away" vs. "will be taken away"; clause structure divergences
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And if they become [wives] to one of the sons of the [other] tribes of the sons of Israel, then their inheritance shall be taken away from the inheritance of our fathers and shall be added to the inheritance of the tribe to which they shall belong, and from the lot of our inheritance it shall be taken away." |
| LOGOS | "And if they become wives to any of the sons of the other tribes of the sons of Israel, then their inheritance will be taken away from the inheritance of our fathers and will be added to the inheritance of the tribe to which they belong; and it will be taken away from the lot of our inheritance." |
| PNEUMA | "And if they marry any of the sons of the other tribes of the sons of Israel, then their inheritance will be taken away from the inheritance of our ancestors and added to the inheritance of the tribe into which they marry, and it will be taken away from the allotment of our inheritance." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Consensus: "And if they become wives to any of the sons of the other tribes of the sons of Israel, then their inheritance shall be taken away from the inheritance of our fathers and shall be added to the inheritance of the tribe to which they shall belong, and from the lot of our inheritance it shall be taken away." -- (1) "become wives" (LOGOS) is adopted over EMET's bracketed "[wives]" and PNEUMA's "marry." The Hebrew l'nashim tihyenah ("become wives") is a technical legal phrase; "marry" (PNEUMA) is idiomatic but loses the Hebrew construction. EMET's brackets around "[wives]" are unnecessary since l'nashim is explicit in the text. (2) "any of the sons" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) is adopted over EMET's "one of the sons" for l'echad mibbnei; echad here means "any one," and "any" conveys the hypothetical nature better. (3) "shall be taken away" (EMET) is adopted over "will be taken away" (LOGOS/PNEUMA); "shall" preserves the legal/consequential force. (4) "our fathers" (EMET/LOGOS) is adopted over "our ancestors" (PNEUMA) for avoteinu, preserving the Hebrew term. (5) EMET preserves the Hebrew clause order with "from the lot of our inheritance it shall be taken away" at the end; LOGOS and PNEUMA restructure. The EMET word order is adopted as it reflects the Hebrew emphasis. (6) "the lot of our inheritance" (EMET/LOGOS) is adopted over "the allotment of our inheritance" (PNEUMA) for goralenu; goral specifically means "lot" (the cast lot). (7) PNEUMA's "into which they marry" is a dynamic rephrasing of the Hebrew asher tihyenah lahem ("to which they shall belong"); the more literal "to which they shall belong" (EMET/LOGOS) is adopted. (8) EMET brackets "[other]" but the Hebrew does have akherot ("other"); removing the brackets.

---

### 36:4 -- "shall be" vs. "comes"; clause structure; "shall be taken away" vs. "will be taken away"; "permanently added" (PNEUMA addition)
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And when the Jubilee of the sons of Israel shall be, then their inheritance shall be added to the inheritance of the tribe to which they shall belong, and from the inheritance of the tribe of our fathers their inheritance shall be taken away." |
| LOGOS | "And when the Jubilee of the sons of Israel comes, then their inheritance will be added to the inheritance of the tribe to which they belong, and their inheritance will be taken away from the inheritance of the tribe of our fathers." |
| PNEUMA | "And when the Jubilee of the sons of Israel comes, their inheritance will be permanently added to the inheritance of the tribe into which they marry, and their inheritance will be taken away from the inheritance of the tribe of our ancestors." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Consensus: "And when the Jubilee of the sons of Israel shall be, then their inheritance shall be added to the inheritance of the tribe to which they shall belong, and from the inheritance of the tribe of our fathers their inheritance shall be taken away." -- (1) "shall be" (EMET) is adopted for yihyeh over "comes" (LOGOS/PNEUMA); the Hebrew uses the verb hayah ("to be"), not bo ("to come"), and "shall be" preserves the literal form. (2) "shall" is adopted throughout over "will" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) for consistency with legal decree language. (3) PNEUMA adds "permanently," which is not in the Hebrew text; this is an interpretive addition and is excluded. (4) "to which they shall belong" (EMET) is adopted over PNEUMA's "into which they marry," per verse 3 resolution. (5) EMET's clause order "from the inheritance of the tribe of our fathers their inheritance shall be taken away" preserves the Hebrew fronting of the prepositional phrase; adopted over the restructured order in LOGOS/PNEUMA. (6) "our fathers" (EMET/LOGOS) adopted over "our ancestors" (PNEUMA) per standing convention.

---

### 36:5 -- "according to the mouth of YHWH" vs. "according to the word of YHWH" vs. "at the word of YHWH"
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And Moses commanded the sons of Israel according to the mouth of YHWH, saying..." |
| LOGOS | "And Moses commanded the sons of Israel according to the word of YHWH, saying..." |
| PNEUMA | "And Moses commanded the sons of Israel at the word of YHWH, saying..." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** "according to the mouth of YHWH" (EMET) is adopted. The Hebrew al-pi YHWH uses pi ("mouth"), not davar ("word"). The "mouth" image is theologically important in Numbers, where Moses is uniquely described as receiving communication "mouth to mouth" (peh el peh, Numbers 12:8). Rendering pi as "word" (LOGOS) or paraphrasing "at the word" (PNEUMA) obscures this. The literal "mouth" is clear in English and preserves the Hebrew anthropomorphism.

---

### 36:6 -- "This [is] the thing which" vs. "This is the thing that" vs. "This is the word that"; "[whoever is] good in their eyes" vs. "whomever is good in their eyes" vs. "whomever they see fit"; "only to a clan... shall they become wives" vs. "only they shall become wives within the clan" vs. "only they must marry within a clan"; "has commanded" vs. "commands"
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "This [is] the thing which YHWH has commanded concerning the daughters of Zelophehad, saying: Let them become wives to [whoever is] good in their eyes, only to a clan of the tribe of their father shall they become wives." |
| LOGOS | "This is the thing that YHWH has commanded concerning the daughters of Zelophehad, saying: Let them become wives to whomever is good in their eyes, only they shall become wives within the clan of the tribe of their father." |
| PNEUMA | "This is the word that YHWH commands concerning the daughters of Zelophehad, saying: Let them marry whomever they see fit -- only they must marry within a clan of the tribe of their father." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Consensus: "This is the thing which YHWH has commanded concerning the daughters of Zelophehad, saying: Let them become wives to whomever is good in their eyes; only to a clan of the tribe of their father shall they become wives." -- (1) "the thing" (EMET/LOGOS) is adopted over "the word" (PNEUMA) for ha-davar; while davar can mean "word," in this juridical context "thing" (i.e., "ruling, matter") is the better sense, and EMET/LOGOS agree. (2) "which" (EMET) is adopted over "that" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) for asher; both are valid, but "which" is slightly more formal for legal proclamation. (3) "has commanded" (EMET/LOGOS, perfect) is adopted over "commands" (PNEUMA, present); the Hebrew tsivvah is a Piel perfect, indicating a completed act of commanding. (4) EMET's brackets around "[is]" and "[whoever is]" are removed; "whomever is good in their eyes" (LOGOS) provides the sense cleanly without brackets. (5) PNEUMA's "whomever they see fit" dynamically paraphrases la-tov b'eineihem; the literal "good in their eyes" (EMET/LOGOS) is adopted. (6) PNEUMA's "marry" is again replaced with "become wives" per the standing convention for l'nashim tihyenah. (7) For the restriction clause, EMET's word order "only to a clan of the tribe of their father shall they become wives" preserves the Hebrew fronting of the prepositional phrase with akh; this is adopted over LOGOS's restructured "only they shall become wives within the clan" and PNEUMA's "only they must marry within a clan." (8) PNEUMA uses "must marry," introducing deontic modality not in the Hebrew; "shall become wives" is the appropriate rendering.

---

### 36:7 -- "shall not turn about" vs. "shall transfer"; "cling" vs. "hold fast"; "each [man]" vs. "each one" vs. "each"
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And the inheritance of the sons of Israel shall not turn about from tribe to tribe, for each [man] of the sons of Israel shall cling to the inheritance of the tribe of his fathers." |
| LOGOS | "And the inheritance of the sons of Israel shall not turn about from tribe to tribe, for each one of the sons of Israel shall cling to the inheritance of the tribe of his fathers." |
| PNEUMA | "And no inheritance belonging to the sons of Israel shall transfer from tribe to tribe, for each of the sons of Israel shall hold fast to the inheritance of the tribe of his ancestors." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Consensus: "And the inheritance of the sons of Israel shall not turn about from tribe to tribe, for each one of the sons of Israel shall cling to the inheritance of the tribe of his fathers." -- (1) "shall not turn about" (EMET/LOGOS) is adopted over "shall transfer" (PNEUMA) for lo tissov; the root savav means "to turn, go around," and "turn about" preserves the Hebrew's rotational image. PNEUMA's "transfer" is interpretive. (2) "the inheritance of the sons of Israel shall not" (EMET/LOGOS) follows the Hebrew subject-verb order; PNEUMA restructures to "no inheritance belonging to the sons of Israel shall." (3) "cling" (EMET/LOGOS) is adopted over "hold fast" (PNEUMA) for yidbaqu; davaq means "to cling, cleave" and "cling" is more literal and preserves the intertextual link to Genesis 2:24. (4) "each one" (LOGOS) is adopted over EMET's bracketed "[man]" and PNEUMA's bare "each"; ish is implied but not explicit, so "each one" conveys the distributive sense without adding a gendered noun. (5) "his fathers" (EMET/LOGOS) adopted over "his ancestors" (PNEUMA) for avotav.

---

### 36:8 -- "possessing" vs. "who possesses" vs. "who inherits a land holding"; "to one of the clan" vs. "to one from the clan" vs. "someone from a clan"; "so that... may possess, each [man]" vs. "so that each one... may possess" vs. "so that each... will possess"
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And every daughter possessing an inheritance from the tribes of the sons of Israel, to one of the clan of the tribe of her father she shall become a wife, so that the sons of Israel may possess, each [man], the inheritance of his fathers." |
| LOGOS | "And every daughter who possesses an inheritance from the tribes of the sons of Israel shall become a wife to one from the clan of the tribe of her father, so that each one of the sons of Israel may possess the inheritance of his fathers." |
| PNEUMA | "And every daughter who inherits a land holding from among the tribes of the sons of Israel shall marry someone from a clan of the tribe of her father, so that each of the sons of Israel will possess the inheritance of his ancestors." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Consensus: "And every daughter possessing an inheritance from the tribes of the sons of Israel shall become a wife to one from the clan of the tribe of her father, so that the sons of Israel may possess, each one, the inheritance of his fathers." -- (1) "possessing" (EMET) is adopted over "who possesses" (LOGOS) and "who inherits" (PNEUMA) for yoreshet; the Hebrew participle yoreshet (feminine of yoresh) functions as an attributive participle, and "possessing" preserves the participial form. PNEUMA's "inherits a land holding" adds "land holding" not in the Hebrew nachalah. (2) EMET's Hebrew-following word order "to one of the clan of the tribe of her father she shall become a wife" preserves the fronted prepositional phrase, but "shall become a wife to one from the clan" (LOGOS) is adopted for the main clause for clarity, while still preserving "become a wife" over PNEUMA's "marry." (3) "to one from the clan" (LOGOS) is adopted over EMET's "to one of the clan" and PNEUMA's "someone from a clan"; l'echad mimmishpachat uses min ("from"), and "from" is more precise. (4) For the purpose clause, EMET's "so that the sons of Israel may possess, each [man]" preserves the Hebrew word order with ish interpolated; adopting "each one" without brackets per verse 7 convention, and preserving the interpolated position. (5) "his fathers" (EMET/LOGOS) over "his ancestors" (PNEUMA).

---

### 36:9 -- "the inheritance shall not turn about" vs. "no inheritance shall turn about" vs. "no inheritance shall transfer"
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And the inheritance shall not turn about from [one] tribe to another tribe, for each [of] the tribes of the sons of Israel shall cling to its own inheritance." |
| LOGOS | "And no inheritance shall turn about from one tribe to another tribe, for each one of the tribes of the sons of Israel shall cling to its own inheritance." |
| PNEUMA | "And no inheritance shall transfer from one tribe to another tribe, for each of the tribes of the sons of Israel shall hold fast to its own inheritance." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Consensus: "And no inheritance shall turn about from one tribe to another tribe, for each of the tribes of the sons of Israel shall cling to its own inheritance." -- (1) "no inheritance shall turn about" (LOGOS) is adopted over EMET's "the inheritance shall not turn about"; the Hebrew v'lo tissov nachalah is literally "and shall not turn about an inheritance," and "no inheritance shall turn about" is natural English that preserves the universal negative. EMET's bracketed "[one]" for the implied "one tribe" is also unnecessary when "one" is adopted from LOGOS. (2) "turn about" (EMET/LOGOS) over "transfer" (PNEUMA), per verse 7 resolution. (3) "cling" (EMET/LOGOS) over "hold fast" (PNEUMA), per verse 7 resolution. (4) "each of the tribes" (PNEUMA) is adopted over EMET's "each [of] the tribes" (brackets) and LOGOS's "each one of the tribes"; the Hebrew ish mattot simply distributes over the tribes, and "each of" is clean.

---

### 36:10 -- "so did the daughters of Zelophehad do" vs. "so the daughters of Zelophehad did"; "As" vs. "Just as"
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "As YHWH commanded Moses, so did the daughters of Zelophehad do." |
| LOGOS | "As YHWH commanded Moses, so the daughters of Zelophehad did." |
| PNEUMA | "Just as YHWH commanded Moses, so the daughters of Zelophehad did." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Consensus: "As YHWH commanded Moses, so did the daughters of Zelophehad do." -- (1) "As" (EMET/LOGOS) is adopted over "Just as" (PNEUMA) for ka'asher; the Hebrew ka'asher is simply "as," and "just as" adds emphasis not in the text. (2) "so did the daughters of Zelophehad do" (EMET) preserves the emphatic ken asu construction with the auxiliary "did...do," which reflects the Hebrew's complete verbal form asu with the emphatic ken. LOGOS/PNEUMA use the simpler "so...did," which is natural English but loses the emphasis of the full verbal construction.

---

### 36:11 -- "sons of their uncles" vs. "sons of their father's brothers"; comma placement
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "And Mahlah, Tirzah, and Hoglah, and Milcah, and Noah, the daughters of Zelophehad, became wives to the sons of their uncles." |
| LOGOS | "And Mahlah, Tirzah, and Hoglah, and Milcah, and Noah, the daughters of Zelophehad, became wives to the sons of their father's brothers." |
| PNEUMA | "And Mahlah, Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Noah, the daughters of Zelophehad, married the sons of their father's brothers." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Consensus: "And Mahlah, Tirzah, and Hoglah, and Milcah, and Noah, the daughters of Zelophehad, became wives to the sons of their uncles." -- (1) "sons of their uncles" (EMET) is adopted over "sons of their father's brothers" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) for livnei dodeihen. The Hebrew dod means "uncle" (father's brother); translating dod as "uncle" is literal and natural, while "father's brothers" is an explanatory circumlocution. (2) "became wives" (EMET/LOGOS) over "married" (PNEUMA) per standing convention for tihyenah l'nashim. (3) The conjunction pattern "Tirzah, and Hoglah, and Milcah, and Noah" (EMET/LOGOS) preserves the Hebrew waw before each name after the first pair; PNEUMA uses standard English list punctuation "Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Noah," dropping the Hebrew conjunctions. The Hebrew-preserving pattern is adopted.

---

### 36:12 -- "To [those] from the clans" vs. "They became wives into the clans" vs. "They married into the clans"; "remained upon" vs. "remained with"; sentence structure
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "To [those] from the clans of the sons of Manasseh son of Joseph they became wives, and their inheritance remained upon the tribe of the clan of their father." |
| LOGOS | "They became wives into the clans of the sons of Manasseh, the son of Joseph, and their inheritance remained with the tribe of the clan of their father." |
| PNEUMA | "They married into the clans of the sons of Manasseh son of Joseph, and their inheritance remained with the tribe of the clan of their father." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Consensus: "To those from the clans of the sons of Manasseh, son of Joseph, they became wives, and their inheritance remained upon the tribe of the clan of their father." -- (1) EMET's fronted prepositional phrase "To [those] from the clans... they became wives" preserves the Hebrew word order (l'mishp'chot... hayu l'nashim); removing the brackets around "[those]" which EMET uses to mark an implied word. However, "To those from" is adopted as it captures the Hebrew min- prefix on l'mishp'chot. (2) "remained upon" (EMET) is adopted over "remained with" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) for al; the Hebrew preposition is al ("upon, on"), not im ("with"). (3) PNEUMA's "married into" again replaces the Hebrew technical phrase with an English idiom; "became wives" is adopted. (4) LOGOS adds "the" before "son of Joseph"; omitted per the convention established in verse 1.

---

### 36:13 -- "the judgments" vs. "the ordinances"; "by the hand of" vs. "through"; "by the Jordan [at] Jericho" vs. "by the Jordan at Jericho" vs. "by the Jordan across from Jericho"; "in the plains" vs. "on the plains"
| Translator | Reading |
|---|---|
| EMET | "These [are] the commandments and the judgments which YHWH commanded by the hand of Moses to the sons of Israel in the plains of Moab by the Jordan [at] Jericho." |
| LOGOS | "These are the commandments and the ordinances that YHWH commanded by the hand of Moses to the sons of Israel in the plains of Moab, by the Jordan at Jericho." |
| PNEUMA | "These are the commandments and the ordinances that YHWH commanded through Moses to the sons of Israel, on the plains of Moab, by the Jordan across from Jericho." |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Consensus: "These are the commandments and the judgments which YHWH commanded by the hand of Moses to the sons of Israel in the plains of Moab, by the Jordan at Jericho." -- (1) "the judgments" (EMET) is adopted over "the ordinances" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) for ha-mishpatim. The Hebrew mishpat means "judgment, legal decision"; "ordinance" is a valid rendering but "judgment" is more literal and captures the juridical character of the content. (2) "which" (EMET) over "that" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) for asher, per verse 6 convention. (3) "by the hand of Moses" (EMET/LOGOS) is adopted over "through Moses" (PNEUMA) for b'yad Mosheh; yad ("hand") is concrete and theologically significant, indicating Moses as the personal instrument of divine legislation. "Through" is a valid dynamic rendering but obscures the "hand" image. (4) EMET's brackets around "[are]" and "[at]" are removed. (5) "in the plains" (EMET/LOGOS) is adopted over "on the plains" (PNEUMA) for b'arvot; the preposition b' is "in," not "on." (6) "by the Jordan at Jericho" (LOGOS) is adopted over PNEUMA's "by the Jordan across from Jericho"; the Hebrew al Yarden Yericho does not include a word for "across from" -- that is an interpretive geographical gloss. The Hebrew places the two names in apposition: "the Jordan of Jericho" or simply "the Jordan at Jericho." (7) EMET omits the comma before "by the Jordan"; adopting the comma (LOGOS/PNEUMA) for readability, as it marks a distinct geographical specification.
